name: Jira Cloud
description: Create, track, and manage remediation tickets in Jira Cloud via the official Atlassian MCP server.
setup_guide_md:
## Jira Cloud integration

OpenSec connects to Jira Cloud using the official [Atlassian MCP server](https://github.com/atlassian/atlassian-mcp-server) (25 tools).

### Prerequisites

1. A Jira Cloud instance (e.g., `yourcompany.atlassian.net`)
2. An **API token** for your Atlassian account

### Create an API token

1. Go to [id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens](https://id.atlassian.com/manage-profile/security/api-tokens)
2. Click **Create API token**
3. Set a label (e.g., `opensec-integration`)
4. Copy the generated token

### What OpenSec uses

- `create_issue` — Create remediation tickets from fix plans
- `update_issue` — Update ticket status and details
- `transition_issue` — Move tickets through workflow states
- `add_comment` — Add remediation progress notes
- `search_issues` — Find related tickets via JQL

### For Jira Server / Data Center

If you're using Jira Server or Data Center (not Cloud), use the `cosmix/jira-mcp` fallback server instead. Same credential format applies.

credentials_schema.label: Atlassian account email
credentials_schema.help_text: The email address associated with your Atlassian account.
